title: Not the answer I was expecting

data.title: Not the answer I was expecting
data.statement:
In French, "say" is pronounced "di" (dis). Therefore, anno-- incredibly funny people, when they hear a word starting with or containing "di", say the rest of the word, as if it was an order. For example, "diriger" (lead) can be heard as "dis riger" (say riger), and therefore they answer "riger".

Unfortunately, you have become one such person. If what the other people says contains a word starting with or containing {{di}} or {{Di}}, <<except for words ending with>> {{di}}<<,>> {{dis}} <<or>> {{dit}}, you must respond with the rest of the word. If there are multiple words in this situation, only process the first one. If there are none, you must respond with {{...}}.

There will <<not>> be any words containing {{di}} multiple times.

testCases.title: One word starting with di
testCases.testIn: J'administre une distillerie proche de Lannion.

testCases.title: One word starting with di
testCases.testIn: Je distribue de la nourriture aux pauvres.

testCases.title: Multiple words starting with di
testCases.testIn: C'est un vrai diablotin, il se dispute en permanence avec son diable de frangin !

testCases.title: Multiple words starting with di
testCases.testIn: Le son subit une distorsion si divine qu'il finit dans une autre dimension !

testCases.title: Word starting with capital D
testCases.testIn: Les allemands utilisent le mot Dienstag pour mardi.

testCases.title: Word starting with capital D
testCases.testIn: C'est un avis de la Direction.

testCases.title: No word containing di
testCases.testIn: Pourquoi tu ne renvoie jamais de message ?

testCases.title: Some dissonnance
testCases.testIn: Je lui dis : tu m'as pris pour un bandit du lundi ?

testCases.title: Some dissonnance
testCases.testIn: Il m'a dit que je lui ai brandit le fait qu'elle lui ai dit !

testCases.title: In the middle
testCases.testIn: Dis donc, c'est assourdissant ton truc, pardi !

testCases.title: In the middle
testCases.testIn: Il n'est pas dit que l'audimat nous soit favorable jeudi.

testCases.title: A bit of everything
testCases.testIn: C'est pas dit que tu brandisse Excalibur un jour, cela va sans dire.

testCases.title: A bit of everything
testCases.testIn: Seul un aventurier hardi qui n'est pas maudit peut dissimuler ceci un mercredi !

data.constraints:
0 <= length([[message]]) < 256
[[message]] is ASCII-compatible, no French weirdness such as accents or cedillas.
data.outputDescription: <<First line:>> Rest of the first word matching the constraints in the original case, or {{...}}.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

In French, "say" is pronounced "di" (dis). Therefore, anno-- incredibly funny people, when they hear a word starting with or containing "di", say the rest of the word, as if it was an order. For example, "diriger" (lead) can be heard as "dis riger" (say riger), and therefore they answer "riger".

Unfortunately, you have become one such person. If what the other people says contains a word starting with or containing di or Di, except for words ending with di, dis or dit, you must respond with the rest of the word. If there are multiple words in this situation, only process the first one. If there are none, you must respond with ....

There will not be any words containing di multiple times.
Input
First line: message
Output
First line: Rest of the first word matching the constraints in the original case, or ....
Constraints
0 <= length(message) < 256
message is ASCII-compatible, no French weirdness such as accents or cedillas.
Example
Input
J'administre une distillerie proche de Lannion.
Output
stillerie
